title: Hello from 1st generation EVA fan
wikitext:
[]: Hello from "1st generation" EVA fan             Quote Post by FinalImpactCrisis » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:36 pm Hello everyone, Believe it or not, I just discovered this community last week and I have been a fan of Evangelion since mid-1997! I first heard of Evangelion back in 1996, this was back when I was in college and using the dejanews groups and anime info was beginning to become easier to find on the internet. People kept talking up some new anime series that was "controversial" and was going to change the world. I laughed but decided to keep an open mind about it. Back in those days, one of the absolute best ways to get new anime was on Elizabeth Street in Chinatown in NYC (it still remains one of the best outlets for anime in the US period); and I went down there to get the first 3 episodes that were released at that point. The quality was horrible, but I was able to make out the basics of the story. I was shocked and surprised that people online were actually being truthful about the show. I didn't become a fan immediately because I wasn't sure which direction the show was going in until much later, but I did get on board before it became "cool" to become an EVA fan. My full conversion happened when I began to work for an anime distribution company in the northeast. We moved hundreds of EVA tapes each month, and this was back when ADV was releasing two eps per tape with several months between each release. I had the opportunity to watch the same two episodes at least 20 times apiece and that definitely helped me to understand that there was a massive story at play. My staff pushed EVA hard to a lot of skeptical anime fans and we know for a fact we converted tons of people into being EVA fans. It was a wonderful time, before the critical mass of anime fandom exploded onto the scene and when fandom was more concerned with quality than cheesy space comedies. In any case, I wanted to say hello to all here, and while I may be a bit older than the average EVA fan (33), I believe that I can have some great discussions here. Take care everyone, FIC

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:37 pm Welcome aboard.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:49 pm Eventually, everyone on the Path of Eva finds their way here....

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:53 pm Welcome aboard indeed! Nice to see someone else who's been around NGE since the beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by FinalImpactCrisis » Sun Dec 02, 2007 2:46 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Welcome aboard indeed! Nice to see someone else who's been around NGE since the beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Dec 02, 2007 2:56 pm I also think it's the differences in the East and West. The East has produced "giant monster" type stuff for a long time. The most we ever had here was King King and terrible old B-sci-fi movies. And of course, robots are a bit thing in Japan especially. So it's only logical that mecha would become a huge genre there.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Dec 02, 2007 3:19 pm Welcome to the forum! Let me introduce you around... Reichu, she's in charge of coming up with crackpot theories. Jimbo, he's in charge of comparing Anno to Orson Welles. Sailor Star Dust, she's in charge of the holy Asuka/Shinji pairing. Ornette, he's in charge of drinking and smoking heavily. And there's myself, who is in charge of never adding anything beneficial to discussions. Enjoy yourself. FinalImpactCrisis wrote: I am wondering if the new series of films will bring in any new fans?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 02, 2007 3:30 pm Welcome aboard, FIC. BrikHaus wrote: Welcome to the forum! Let me introduce you around...

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Dec 02, 2007 3:33 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I'm not sure whether to be glad or upset that I didn't net mentioned, as the token dirty old man

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Dec 02, 2007 4:41 pm BrikHaus wrote: Jimbo, he's in charge of comparing Anno to Orson Welles.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 02, 2007 4:59 pm What about Shinji's hand falling on Rei's boob in episode 5? Now THAT'S deep meaning! (Actually according to the ancient Eva Commentary, somebody said that breasts are "of thematic importance in Eva." Makes sense, given various examples including Asuka in EoE.) @ Brik-kun: You forgot about Maggot Master...who's in charge of...well, being Maggot Master.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Sun Dec 02, 2007 5:10 pm hehs. Welcome aboard. I am interested to have someone around who has been here since the beginning. I also recently purchased ancient English tapes (fan for six months now).

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Dec 02, 2007 5:31 pm Also, there's Bob-bq, who's our resident sage in politics, electronics, the military, and pretty much all things related to weaponry, as well as the author of the current favourite fanfic on the boards, Tenant of room 404. Me? I'm just another nut who saw parallels between himself and Shinji. Maybe I'll fill this site's role as fanfic-critic (as well as the only other person who beleives in the Asuka/Kaoru pairing besides the great Eva-monkey) Anyway that's enough about me, the young wipper-snapper, so welcome to the board! p.s Maggotmaster's our resident mystery-man. We all want to know who he/she is, half of us think it's Reichu, and the sane ones know that that's a load of bollocks. :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Dec 02, 2007 5:32 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: (Actually according to the ancient Eva Commentary, somebody said that breasts are "of thematic importance in Eva." Makes sense, given various examples including Asuka in EoE.)

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Dec 02, 2007 5:45 pm I'm Chee, and I'm the resident gnome in your skull.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 02, 2007 5:53 pm @ Yojimbo: Or in this case: Leaving Mother, the First Other=Significant Other. :mrgreen:

[]:             Quote Post by FinalImpactCrisis » Sun Dec 02, 2007 6:55 pm HAHAHAAHAH :P You folks are funny. Thanks for the warm welcome here. In terms of my personality towards EVA: a) I think Asuka and Shinji did get it on at some point. Not sex, but definitely something physical and mutual. b) I believe that Shinji did reject Instrumentality and that the world was set to "normal" at the end of EoE. c) Misato was awesome and bi-sexual. There is some evidence to support this. :shock: d) I didn't care for all the cutesy comedy stuff in the first 8 episodes. It just bothered me to no end when the cheesy middle school stuff would pop up. Sorry. e) I also believe that the actions of NERV, SEELE, and Gendo should have caused all of them to burn in hell as opposed to Instrumentality. f) I still wonder if the angels were truly "biblical" or just aliens who used the Impacts to colonize worlds across the galaxy. That's just some of my base beliefs in the series. FIC

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Sun Dec 02, 2007 7:27 pm @ F) The Angels weren't angels at all. They were organisms spawned by Adam, the first Seed of Life, whose White Moon landed on Earth before Lilith's Black Moon. Lilith's took priority over Adam's, and Adam got a Spear of Longinus in the chest, until it was removed by the Katsuragi Expedition, thus instigating 2nd Impact. Anyway, I'm Shiro. Resident Eva newb, catching up fast. Also the second member of the official Eva fangeeks Audio Commentary. (Jimbo's the first)

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Dec 02, 2007 7:42 pm FinalImpactCrisis wrote: c) Misato was awesome and bi-sexual. There is some evidence to support this. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 02, 2007 7:44 pm FinalImpactCrisis wrote: c) Misato was awesome and bi-sexual. There is some evidence to support this. :shock:

I first heard of Evangelion back in 1996, this was back when I was in college and using the dejanews groups and anime info was beginning to become easier to find on the internet. People kept talking up some new anime series that was "controversial" and was going to change the world. I laughed but decided to keep an open mind about it. Back in those days, one of the absolute best ways to get new anime was on Elizabeth Street in Chinatown in NYC (it still remains one of the best outlets for anime in the US period); and I went down there to get the first 3 episodes that were released at that point. The quality was horrible, but I was able to make out the basics of the story. I was shocked and surprised that people online were actually being truthful about the show. I didn't become a fan immediately because I wasn't sure which direction the show was going in until much later, but I did get on board before it became "cool" to become an EVA fan. My full conversion happened when I began to work for an anime distribution company in the northeast. We moved hundreds of EVA tapes each month, and this was back when ADV was releasing two eps per tape with several months between each release. I had the opportunity to watch the same two episodes at least 20 times apiece and that definitely helped me to understand that there was a massive story at play. My staff pushed EVA hard to a lot of skeptical anime fans and we know for a fact we converted tons of people into being EVA fans. It was a wonderful time, before the critical mass of anime fandom exploded onto the scene and when fandom was more concerned with quality than cheesy space comedies. In any case, I wanted to say hello to all here, and while I may be a bit older than the average EVA fan (33), I believe that I can have some great discussions here. Take care everyone, FIC
